title: pyrus cambrica
intro: Pyrus cambrica, also known as wild pear or crab apple, is a small deciduous tree or shrub native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Pyrus cambrica include reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and acting as a natural antihistamine. Pyrus cambrica is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in salves and ointments for topical application.
